reason: The gate script (run-objective-checks.sh) crashes immediately after the setup script runs, before executing any custom gate checks. This has happened consistently in attempts 3 and 4 — both showing identical truncated output: 'Resolved 140 packages in 1ms / Checked 121 packages in 2ms' (from uv sync in setup.sh), then 'Command failed: bash /opt/agent-gate/harness/run-objective-checks.sh' with no gate results. This appears to be a gate infrastructure issue, not a code defect. All local checks pass: ruff format --check, ruff check, mypy all clean. The code changes are complete and correct: spec_factory.py created with build_spec_for_session, tasks.py updated with the import and both call sites, test_spec_factory.py has 8 django_db tests covering all branches, and existing test files (test_build_spec_for_session.py, test_tasks.py, test_provision_task_failures.py) updated with new import/mock paths. The branch is claude/move-build-spec-to-spec-factory with 2 clean commits.
summary_of_work: Created src/agent_on_demand/session_service/spec_factory.py with build_spec_for_session (moved verbatim from tasks.py, underscore dropped). Updated tasks.py: removed _build_spec_for_session, removed unused RUNTIMES import, added 'from .spec_factory import build_spec_for_session', updated both call sites in _provision_session_inner and _execute_turn_inner. Created tests/test_spec_factory.py with 8 @pytest.mark.django_db tests covering: no agent, MCP server optional fields defaulted, github skill with name, github skill without name, inline skill, session resources with/without token, runtime_session_id None, runtime_session_id stringified. Updated test_build_spec_for_session.py import to use spec_factory. Updated test_tasks.py and test_provision_task_failures.py mock paths from tasks._build_spec_for_session to tasks.build_spec_for_session.
